rationale: Determinism check is mathematically infeasible for receive_packet's spec. The spec is a disjunction of two cases: (A) record the packet via SingleDelivery::receive when pre.received_packet is None, and (B) drop the packet (post == pre, out == empty) when pre.received_packet is Some OR okay_to_ignore_packets() is true. Since okay_to_ignore_packets() unconditionally returns true (its body is `true`), Case B is always available even when pre.received_packet is None. Therefore for the same pre-state two distinct valid outcomes exist: post.received_packet = Some(pkt) with a non-empty sent_packets (Case A) vs post == pre with empty sent_packets (Case B). host_state_common_postconditions does not break the tie (both cases satisfy it). det_host_model_receive_packet_equal demands strict equality on (sent_packets, ack.dst/src/msg, post.view()), which fails across the two disjuncts. Tried reveal(okay_to_ignore_packets) and direct assert(false); both confirm the non-determinism. The original gener
